id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, and created_at.
After Argon2id verification, if enabled, it requires TOTP or one of 10 single-use recovery codes.
10 recovery codes are generated; they are single-use and permanently consumed upon use.
Exponential backoff with 5 attempts over 24 hours.

At rest: PII is protected by AES-256-GCM with per-tenant keys, and passwords use Argon2id. In transit: mTLS via Envoy and RDS connections use sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem. Secrets are rotated every 90 days.

The Gateway validates the schema; the Lambda-authorizer validates the JWT via JWKS and injects claims; the request passes VPCLink to the ALB; the Auth Service handlePostLogin verifies the password via Argon2id and checks MFA (TOTP/recovery code); finally, it issues RS256 access and refresh tokens.

Patterns: handle{Verb}{Resource} naming, RFC7807 error format, testcontainers for PG, Pino logging, and Claude Code review. Constraints: JWKS auth validation, 20 connections/instance pool, and 1MB ALB payload limit.
The request will be throttled at the ALB layer, which has a rate limit of 1000/min per IP.
The Auth Service (for issuance using the private key), the Gateway Lambda-authorizer (for validation via JWKS), and all downstream services like Portfolio and Notification (for validation via JWKS).

GitHub Actions handles linting, typechecking, testing, building, and pushing to ECR/ECS. A Claude Code review is required before merging. Deployment is zero-downtime using a blue-green ALB target switch.
